subsections.label: a
subsections.text: The department shall establish and administer a Safe Routes to School Program to distribute money received under the Hazard Elimination Program (23 U.S.C. Section 152), as amended, to political subdivisions for projects to improve safety in and around school areas. Projects eligible to receive money under this program may include:
installation of new crosswalks and bike lanes;
implementation of traffic-calming programs in neighborhoods around schools; and
construction of wide outside lanes to be used as bike routes.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The department, in considering project proposals under this section, shall consider:
the demonstrated need of the applicant;
the potential of the proposal to reduce child injuries and fatalities;
the potential of the proposal to encourage walking and bicycling among students;
identification of current and potential walking and bicycling routes to school; and
support for the projects proposed by local school-based associations, traffic engineers, elected officials, law enforcement agencies, and school officials.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: The department may allocate money received by the department from the federal government under the Hazard Elimination Program (23 U.S.C. Section 152), as amended, to projects under this section.

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: The department shall adopt rules to implement this section.

history.raw: Added by Acts 2001, 77th Leg., ch. 1085, Sec. 3, eff. Sept. 1, 2001.
